user_request: We're quoting a mid-size corporate office expansion for a client adding 150 desks to their existing campus. They need 24-port managed PoE switches for the new floor, a next-generation firewall to handle increased perimeter traffic, and Cat6A structured cabling to support the buildout. Budget is flexible but the client prefers not to overspend on switching. Please generate a full 3-tier BOM.

budget.summary: Budget tiers minimize unit cost while meeting all rule thresholds. The switching budget pick carries a stale-data warning from Juniper Networks that the SE must acknowledge before finalizing.
bullets: networking-switch: VEN-002/V002-SW-0001 (Juniper Networks) at $1,055.74 — lowest-cost qualifying 24-port PoE+ managed switch in the filtered catalog
Lead-time compliance (rule-001): 10d — within the 21d threshold; no substitution required
Margin compliance (rule-004): 16.2% — above the 15.0% floor; no flag triggered
rule-002 flag: Juniper Networks effective_date 2026-02-18 is 82d old, exceeding the 60d freshness threshold — requires SE acknowledgment before customer delivery
network-security: VEN-009/V009-SEC-0002 (Check Point Software) at $666.22 — branch-tier NGFW (1-5Gbps), lowest-cost NGFW candidate with no rules fired and 27.3% margin
structured-cabling: VEN-020/V020-CAB-0004 (Belden) at $457.89 — lowest-cost clean 48-port Cat6A patch panel, 2d lead time, 20.4% margin

balanced.summary: Balanced tiers select mid-cost, rule-clean SKUs that align with the SE's guidance that the client prefers not to overspend on switching. All three balanced line items carry no rule flags.
bullets: networking-switch: VEN-043/V043-SW-0001 (APC by Schneider) at $1,401.03 — clean 24-port PoE+ managed switch, 14d lead time, aligned with cost-conscious switching direction
Lead-time compliance (rule-001): 14d — within the 21d threshold; no substitution required
Margin compliance (rule-004): 18.4% — above the 15.0% floor; no flag triggered
No rule-002, rule-003, or rule-004 flags on any balanced line item across all three categories
network-security: VEN-041/V041-SEC-0002 (Black Box Corporation) at $5,231.05 — enterprise-edge NGFW (6-15Gbps) stepping up from branch-tier budget; appropriate for 150-desk perimeter expansion
structured-cabling: VEN-021/V021-CAB-0001 (Legrand) at $473.79 — established structured-cabling brand, 3d lead time; trade-off vs. budget is a modest $15.90 premium for Legrand's enterprise pedigree

premium.summary: Premium tiers maximize performance and delivery speed. The switching premium remains cost-disciplined at $1,606.72 — only 14.7% above the balanced pick — reflecting the SE's guidance to avoid over-investment in switching.
bullets: networking-switch: VEN-041/V041-SW-0001 (Black Box Corporation) at $1,606.72 — fastest lead time of all clean 24-port PoE+ candidates at 2d, 21.0% margin
Lead-time compliance (rule-001): 2d — well within the 21d threshold; no substitution required
Margin compliance (rule-004): 21.0% — above the 15.0% floor; no flag triggered
No rule flags on any premium line item; all three premium SKUs are fully clean
network-security: VEN-009/V009-SEC-0001 (Check Point Software) at $10,472.55 — enterprise-edge NGFW (6-15Gbps) with highest margin of clean enterprise candidates at 28.2% and 4d lead time

structured-cabling: VEN-024/V024-CAB-0002 (Vertiv) at $682.11 — highest-cost clean patch panel with best margin (20.5%) and 3d lead time; trade-off vs. balanced is a $208.32 step-up for Vertiv's data-center-grade pedigree

soft_recommendation: For the 150-desk campus expansion, the recommended configuration is: balanced switching (V043-SW-0001, $1,401.03/unit) to honor the client's preference not to overspend, premium NGFW (V009-SEC-0001, $10,472.55) to ensure the enterprise-edge throughput (6-15Gbps) needed for the expanded perimeter, and balanced structured cabling (V021-CAB-0001, $473.79/panel) as a clean, name-brand infrastructure anchor. This cross-tier configuration delivers a blended margin of approximately 22.4% across the three anchor line items. The only advisory to resolve before delivery is the stale-data warning on the budget switching alternative (Juniper Networks, VEN-002, 82d since last price confirmation) — the balanced and premium switch picks carry no such flags and are ready to present.
